fault_hypothesis: Lines 72-73 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams share the same last value, but this deduplication logic is wrong — when both streams end with the same value, both copies should be preserved in the output, not one removed.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid merged output — when both streams share a last value, both copies must remain.
After the conditional pop, the merged list should still contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements since no deduplication should occur.
At each loop iteration end, the number of merged elements should equal the sum of consumed indices from both streams.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both input streams combined.
